sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Important finding! After clicking on the red block and pressing RIGHT:
- The red block didn't move (still at [[30, 12], [30, 17], [33, 17], [33, 12]])
- But the GREEN square moved! It went from [[38, 10], [38, 11], [39, 11], [39, 10]] to [[38, 12], [38, 13], [39, 13], [39, 12]]
So clicking on the red block didn't select it - the arrow keys still control the green square. The green square moved from cols 10-11 to cols 12-13.
Wait, but the green square is at cols 12-13 which is at the right edge of the container. The container walls are at cols 8-9 and 12-13. So the green square is now touching the right wall.
Let me think about this more carefully. The green square is 2x2. The container at rows 38-39 has walls at cols 8-9 and 12-13, so the interior is cols 10-11 (2 cols wide). The green square fits exactly in cols 10-11.
But now the green square is at cols 12-13, which is where the walls are! This means either:
1. The green square moved outside the container
2. The walls at cols 12-13 are not actually walls at rows 38-39
Let me check the ASCII again to see what's at rows 38-39, cols 12-13.
Actually, looking at the ASCII from before:
Row 38: ccggNNggccc (cols 8-9 are g, 10-11 are N, 12-13 are g)
But now the green square is at cols 12-13. So either the ASCII changed or the green square replaced the gray wall pixels.
Let me check the current ASCII to see what happened.
Actually, I think the green square moved to cols 12-13, replacing the gray pixels there. This means the container's right wall at rows 38-39 is not a solid barrier - the green square can move through it!
This is a key insight! The green square can escape the container by moving to certain positions. Let me explore this further.
Wait, but earlier when I tried moving the green square UP from rows 40-41, it went to rows 38-39 (the top opening). Then I tried UP again and it didn't go further. So the top wall at row 38 IS a barrier.
But now the green square moved RIGHT from cols 10-11 to cols 12-13, which was previously a gray wall. So the right wall at rows 38-39 is NOT a barrier.
This suggests the container has an opening on the right side at rows 38-39. Let me verify by looking at the current ASCII.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current ASCII around the green square
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Current state around green square (rows 36-45, cols 6-16):")
for i in range(36, 45):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][6:17]}")

# The green square is now at cols 12-13. Let me try moving it further right
print("\nTrying RIGHT again...")
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green after RIGHT: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        break

# Check ASCII again
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nAfter RIGHT (rows 36-45, cols 6-16):")
for i in range(36, 45):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][6:17]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
